name: Finder Selection Relay
short_description: A menu-bar relay for turning the current Finder selection into clean paths and handoff-ready context.
audience: Mac users moving Finder selections into chat, notes, tickets, or terminal commands
Developers and operators who need exact local paths without Finder cleanup
People who hand off files and folders across apps all day
detail_lines: Reads the current Finder selection through macOS automation and can fall back to explicit local paths without opening file contents or using the network.
Formats selected files and folders for prompt context, markdown notes, ticket bullets, or shell-safe path lists in one step.
Keeps the handoff small and menu-bar first so chosen Finder items move cleanly into the next tool.
